name: Rippling Platform API
what_it_does: REST API for Rippling's all-in-one HR, payroll, and IT management platform enabling programmatic employee lifecycle management, payroll data access, and app provisioning.
use_cases: Automating employee onboarding and offboarding workflows from agent pipelines
Syncing headcount and org structure data to downstream analytics systems
Triggering IT provisioning and deprovisioning on employment status changes
Extracting payroll and compensation data for finance reporting
not_for: Companies not using Rippling for HR/payroll
Standalone payroll processing (requires full Rippling subscription)
High-frequency real-time event processing — Rippling API is not optimized for event streaming
best_when: Your organization uses Rippling as its HRIS and you want to automate employee lifecycle events, sync people data across systems, or trigger IT provisioning from HR state changes.
avoid_when: You need a best-of-breed ATS API for recruiting workflows — Rippling's ATS is weaker than Greenhouse or Lever; use Rippling API for HRIS data, not recruiting pipeline management.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 required for all API access. Applications must be registered as Rippling integrations. Scopes control access to HR, payroll, IT, and identity data. API access on Rippling Unity tier and above.

pricing.paid_tiers: Rippling base platform starts around $8/employee/month. API access requires Unity tier or higher. Actual API pricing is not separately itemized — bundled with the platform subscription.
pricing.notes: No public API-specific pricing. API access gates behind higher-tier subscription. All plans require a sales engagement.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits vary by endpoint. Specific limits shared during integration onboarding. Rippling recommends batch operations over polling for bulk data retrieval.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses vary in quality across endpoints. Common errors return structured JSON, but some edge case errors return generic messages without actionable codes. Documentation of specific error scenarios is sparse.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency key support documented. Agents should implement duplicate-check logic before write operations, particularly for employee record creation.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth token refresh complexity is high — tokens expire and must be refreshed; failure to refresh causes silent failures in long-running agent workflows.
API access is gated behind the Unity tier; agents deployed to organizations on lower tiers will receive authorization errors.
Employee data model is deeply hierarchical and requires multiple API calls to assemble a complete employee record across HR, payroll, and IT modules.
Rate limits are not publicly documented; agents operating at scale should coordinate expected throughput with Rippling during integration review.
Webhook events do not cover all entity changes — some HR state changes must be detected via polling rather than event subscription.

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC 2 Type II certified. OAuth 2.0 with scopes provides the strongest auth model in the HR/ATS category evaluated. Payroll data access is separately scoped. Domain verification required for integration registration.
